title: CIL Civil — Full-length Mock 01
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: As per Income Tax Department data (June 2026), India's net direct tax collections in early FY27 rose by about what percentage year-on-year?
questions.solution: Net direct tax collections rose ~14.6% YoY to about Rs 5.21 trillion.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, which country became the first Central Asian member of the New Development Bank (NDB)?
questions.solution: Uzbekistan became the first Central Asian nation to join the BRICS-backed NDB.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which India-born theoretical physicist won the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics?
questions.solution: Rajasthan-born physicist Jainendra K. Jain received the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution ranked first in the QS World University Rankings 2027 (released June 2026)?
questions.solution: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) ranked first in QS World University Rankings 2027.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In June 2026, which Indian jurist was elected a Judge of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)?
questions.solution: Indian jurist Bimal N. Patel was elected to ITLOS in June 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, PM Narendra Modi received the highest national award of which country?
questions.solution: PM Modi was conferred Slovakia's highest national award in 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026, in which an Indian Army contingent participated, was held in which country?
questions.solution: The multinational Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026 was held in Mongolia.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was named the new Chairman of GIC Re in June 2026?
questions.solution: The government named Hitesh Joshi as the new Chairman of GIC Re.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, ONGC was assigned to develop a new strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) facility at which location?
questions.solution: ONGC was tasked with building a new underground crude oil SPR cavern at Mangaluru, Karnataka.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The India–UK CETA and Social Security Agreement were reported to take effect from which date?
questions.solution: Reports indicated the India–UK CETA would take effect from July 15, 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The headquarters of Coal India Limited (CIL) is located in which city?
questions.solution: Coal India Limited is headquartered in Kolkata, West Bengal.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.solution: Canberra is the capital of Australia, not Sydney or Melbourne.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the largest planet in the Solar System?
questions.solution: Jupiter is the largest planet by both mass and diameter.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who chaired the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution?
questions.solution: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar chaired the Drafting Committee of the Constitution.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the national animal of India?
questions.solution: The Royal Bengal Tiger is the national animal of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the chemical symbol of Gold?
questions.solution: Gold's symbol is Au (from Latin 'aurum'); Ag is silver.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the longest river in India?
questions.solution: The Ganga is the longest river flowing within India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was the first President of India?
questions.solution: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President of India (1950–62).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the currency of Japan?
questions.solution: The Yen is the currency of Japan; Yuan is Chinese, Won is Korean.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: How many Fundamental Rights are currently guaranteed by the Indian Constitution?
questions.solution: There are six Fundamental Rights after the Right to Property was removed in 1978.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Taj Mahal is located in which city?
questions.solution: The Taj Mahal is in Agra, Uttar Pradesh, on the bank of the Yamuna.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which gas is most abundant in Earth's atmosphere?
questions.solution: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the atmosphere by volume.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is India's national space agency?
questions.solution: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is India's space agency.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In which year did the Dandi (Salt) March take place?
questions.solution: Gandhi led the Dandi Salt March in 1930 during the Civil Disobedience Movement.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the largest coal-producing state in India in recent years?
questions.solution: Chhattisgarh has been India's largest coal-producing state, ahead of Odisha and Jharkhand.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A vessel holds 100 L of milk. 20 L is drawn out and replaced with water; this is repeated 3 times. The milk remaining is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each draw-and-replace keeps the fraction $(1-x/V)$ of milk; after n repeats this fraction is raised to the n-th power.
**Formula.** $M=V\left(1-\tfrac{x}{V}\right)^{n}$
**Given.** V = 100 L, x = 20 L, n = 3.
**Working.** $=100\left(1-\tfrac{20}{100}\right)^{3}$.
**Answer.** $M\approx 51.2$ L.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two trains 180 m and 130 m long move in opposite directions at 54 km/h and 36 km/h. The time to cross each other is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Opposite directions add speeds; convert km/h to m/s with the factor 5/18, then divide the combined length by the relative speed.
**Formula.** $t=\tfrac{L_1+L_2}{(s_1+s_2)\times\frac{5}{18}}$
**Given.** L₁ = 180 m, L₂ = 130 m, s₁ = 54 km/h, s₂ = 36 km/h.
**Working.** Relative $=(54+36)\times\tfrac{5}{18}=25$ m/s; $t=\tfrac{180+130}{25}$.
**Answer.** $t=12.4$ s.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The difference between compound and simple interest on Rs 10000 at 10% per annum for 2 years is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Over 2 years the gap between compound and simple interest is the first year's interest-on-interest.
**Formula.** $CI-SI=P\left(\tfrac{r}{100}\right)^2$
**Given.** P = Rs 10000, r = 10% p.a., t = 2 yr.
**Working.** $=10000\left(\tfrac{10}{100}\right)^2$.
**Answer.** $CI-SI=$ Rs 100.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat covers 72 km downstream in 6 h and the same 72 km upstream in 12 h. Its speed in still water is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Downstream speed adds the stream; upstream subtracts it. Still-water speed is the average of the two.
**Formula.** $u=\tfrac12\left[(b+s)+(b-s)\right]$
**Given.** Downstream 72 km in 6 h; upstream 72 km in 12 h.
**Working.** Downstream $=\tfrac{72}{6}=12$ km/h, upstream $=\tfrac{72}{12}=6$ km/h.
**Answer.** Still-water speed $u=\tfrac{12+6}{2}=9$ km/h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A pipe fills a tank in 8 h, but with a leak it takes 10 h. The leak alone can empty the full tank in:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The leak's rate is the fill-rate without the leak minus the slower fill-rate with the leak.
**Formula.** $T=\tfrac{ab}{b-a}$
**Given.** Fill without leak a = 8 h; with leak b = 10 h.
**Working.** Leak rate $=\tfrac1{8}-\tfrac1{10}$; emptying time $=\tfrac{8\times10}{10-8}$.
**Answer.** $T=40$ h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A trader marks goods 50% above cost, then gives a 25% discount. His net profit percentage is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Net selling factor is the markup factor times the discount factor; profit% is that factor reduced by 1.
**Formula.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{m}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{d}{100}\right)$
**Given.** Markup m = 50%, discount d = 25%.
**Working.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{50}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{25}{100}\right)=1.13$.
**Answer.** Profit $=(f-1)\times100=12.5\%$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A can do a work in 12 days and B in 18 days. Working together they finish it in:
questions.solution: Combined rate = 1/12 + 1/18 = 5/36; time = 36/5 = 7.2 days.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 25% of a number is 50, the number is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An item bought for Rs 800 is sold for Rs 920. The profit percentage is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A car covers 240 km at 60 km/h. The time taken is:
questions.solution: Time = distance/speed = 240/60 = 4 hours.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Pipe A fills a tank in 6 h and pipe B in 4 h. Together they fill it in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The HCF of 36 and 48 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The LCM of 12 and 15 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The square root of 2025 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The value of 7! / 5! is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A sum doubles itself in 8 years under simple interest. The annual rate is:
questions.solution: To double, SI = P, so rate = 100/T = 100/8 = 12.5%.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The average age of 5 persons is 30 years. If a person aged 40 leaves, the average of the remaining 4 is:
questions.solution: Total = 150; remaining = 150 − 40 = 110; average = 110/4 = 27.5.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: 0.25 expressed as a fraction is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The percentage increase when a value rises from 50 to 65 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A number divided by 7 gives quotient 12 and remainder 3. The number is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The compound ratio of 2 : 3 and 4 : 5 is:
questions.solution: Compound ratio = (2×4) : (3×5) = 8 : 15.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An article sold at Rs 450 incurs a 10% loss. Its cost price is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 10 men complete a work in 12 days, 8 men will complete it in:
questions.solution: Men-days constant: 10×12 = 8×D, D = 120/8 = 15 days.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The probability of getting an even number on a single throw of a fair die is:
questions.solution: Even outcomes {2,4,6} = 3 of 6, probability = 1/2.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Successive discounts of 10% and 20% on a marked price of Rs 1000 give a final price of:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 9 km towards north, then turns east and walks 12 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: Straight-line distance = √(9² + 12²) = √225 = 15 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a certain code, each letter of a word is moved two steps forward in the English alphabet. The code for 'FISH' is:
questions.solution: Replace every letter by the one two places ahead: FISH → HKUJ.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'FAD'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of F, A, D to get 11.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution: Tracing each relationship step by step gives: Mother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term in the series: 2, 5, 11, 23, 47, ?
questions.solution: Each term = previous × 2 + 1, so the next term = 47 × 2 + 1 = 95.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 5, 10, 20, 40, ?
questions.solution: Each term doubles, so next = 40 × 2 = 80.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution: The only daughter of her mother is the woman herself, so she is the man's mother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: Z, X, V, T, ?
questions.solution: Letters decrease by 2 each time: T − 2 = R.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: Apple, Mango, Banana, Potato?
questions.solution: Apple, mango and banana are fruits; potato is a vegetable.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If each letter is replaced by the next letter, ROSE is coded as:
questions.solution: R→S, O→P, S→T, E→F, giving SPTF.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 7, 14, 28, 56, ?
questions.solution: Each term doubles, so next = 56 × 2 = 112.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 1st January is a Monday, what day is 8th January?
questions.solution: 8 − 1 = 7 days later, exactly one week, so it is again Monday.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 100, 96, 88, 76, 60, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: 2 : 8 :: 3 : ? (pattern n : n³)

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 3 km north, then 4 km east. His distance from the start is:
questions.solution: Resultant = √(3² + 4²) = √25 = 5 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: AB, DE, GH, ?
questions.solution: Each pair skips one letter: after GH comes JK.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: The angle between the hands of a clock at 3:00 is:
questions.solution: Each hour = 30°; at 3:00 the hands are 3 × 30 = 90° apart.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of 20 people, X is 7th from the left. X's position from the right is:
questions.solution: Position from right = 20 − 7 + 1 = 14.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.solution: Each number is halved, so 16 → 8.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 27, 64, 100, 125?
questions.solution: 27, 64, 125 are perfect cubes (3³, 4³, 5³); 100 is not.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: All roses are flowers. All flowers fade. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution: Roses ⊂ flowers and all flowers fade, so all roses fade.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 3, 6, 11, 18, 27, ?
questions.solution: Differences are 3, 5, 7, 9, 11; 27 + 11 = 38.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A=1, B=2, ... then the sum of the letters of BAD is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which of these fractions is the largest?
questions.solution: As decimals: 0.5, 0.667, 0.75, 0.8; the largest is 4/5.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: M, N, O, P, ?
questions.solution: Consecutive letters of the alphabet; after P comes Q.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Abundant'.
questions.solution: 'Abundant' means existing in large quantity, i.e. plentiful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Ancient'.
questions.solution: 'Ancient' (very old) is opposite to 'modern'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.solution: The correct spelling is 'Occasion' (one c, two s).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'She is good ___ mathematics.'
questions.solution: The idiom is 'good at' a subject or skill.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a person who cannot read or write':
questions.solution: Such a person is 'illiterate'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to break the ice' means:
questions.solution: 'Break the ice' means to initiate conversation in a tense or new setting.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Brief'.
questions.solution: 'Brief' means short or concise.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Victory'.
questions.solution: 'Victory' is opposite to 'defeat'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He has been living here ___ 2010.'
questions.solution: 'Since' is used with a point of time like a year.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Spot the error: 'He / don't / like / coffee.'
questions.solution: Third-person singular needs 'doesn't', not 'don't'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The plural of 'Child' is:
questions.solution: 'Child' has the irregular plural 'children'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Passive voice of 'John wrote the letter.' is:
options: The letter was written by John
The letter is written by John
questions.solution: Past simple active becomes 'was written' in the passive.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He is ___ honest man.'
questions.solution: 'Honest' starts with a vowel sound, so 'an' is used.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The study of living organisms is called:
questions.solution: 'Biology' is the study of all living organisms.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Happy'.
questions.solution: 'Happy' is synonymous with 'joyful'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Expand'.
questions.solution: 'Expand' is opposite to 'contract'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'a piece of cake' means:
questions.solution: 'A piece of cake' means something very easy to do.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Neither he nor I ___ wrong.'
questions.solution: With 'neither...nor', the verb agrees with the nearer subject 'I', so 'am'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.solution: The correct spelling is 'Definitely'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Enormous'.
questions.solution: 'Enormous' means very large, i.e. huge.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Generous'.
questions.solution: 'Generous' is opposite to 'stingy' (mean with money).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One who studies stars and celestial bodies is an:
questions.solution: An 'astronomer' scientifically studies celestial bodies.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Divide this ___ two parts.'
questions.solution: 'Divide into' is the correct collocation for separation.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'once in a blue moon' means:
questions.solution: 'Once in a blue moon' means something happening very rarely.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correct form: 'I ___ my homework already.'
questions.solution: 'Already' signals present perfect, so 'have done' is correct.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 80 MPa and 20 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 80 MPa, σ₃ = 20 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{80+20}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=50$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 20 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 20 kN/m, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{20\times4^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=40$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 20 kPa and friction angle φ = 25°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 100 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 20 kPa, φ = 25°, σ = 100 kPa.
**Working.** $=20+100\times\tan 25^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=66.63$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a central point load of 10 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 10 kN, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{10\times4}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=10$ kNm at mid-span.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 5 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 5 kN/m over its entire length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a cantilever produces its maximum moment at the fixed support.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{2}$
**Given.** w = 5 kN/m, L = 5 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{5\times5^2}{2}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=62.5$ kNm at the fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 130 MPa and 30 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 130 MPa, σ₃ = 30 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{130-30}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=50$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 80 MPa, σy = 40 MPa and τxy = 20 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 80 MPa, σy = 40 MPa, τxy = 20 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=60$, radius $=28.28$; $\sigma_1=60+28.28$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=88.28$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 50 MPa and 10 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 50 MPa, σ₃ = 10 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{50+10}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=30$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M30 concrete, width 230 mm, effective depth 500 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 30 MPa, b = 230 mm, d = 500 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times30\times230\times500^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 238.1$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular channel 5 m wide carries 6 m³/s. The critical depth (yc = (q²/g)^{1/3}) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Critical depth in a rectangular channel depends only on the discharge per unit width q.
**Formula.** $y_c=\left(\tfrac{q^2}{g}\right)^{1/3}$
**Given.** Q = 6 m³/s, B = 5 m, g = 9.81 m/s².
**Working.** $q=\tfrac{6}{5}=1.2$ m²/s; $y_c=\left(\tfrac{1.2^2}{9.81}\right)^{1/3}$.
**Answer.** $y_c\approx 0.53$ m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 8 m carries two equal 10 kN point loads placed 1.5 m from each support. The maximum bending moment (constant between the loads) is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two equal symmetric loads give equal reactions; between the loads the bending moment is constant at $R\cdot a$.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=P\,a$
**Given.** P = 10 kN, a = 1.5 m, span = 8 m.
**Working.** Each reaction $=10$ kN; $M_{max}=10\times1.5$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=15$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-ended column (E = 200 GPa, I = 8×10⁻⁶ m⁴, length 5 m) has an Euler buckling load of about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A pin-ended column buckles at the Euler critical load with effective length equal to the actual length.
**Formula.** $P_{cr}=\tfrac{\pi^2 E I}{L^2}$
**Given.** E = 200 GPa, I = 8×10⁻⁶ m⁴, L = 5 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{\pi^2\times200\times10^9\times8\times10^{-6}}{5^2}$ N.
**Answer.** $P_{cr}\approx 631.7$ kN.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of length 4 m carries a 8 kN load at its free end (E = 200 GPa, I = 8×10⁻⁶ m⁴). The tip deflection (WL³/3EI) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An end-loaded cantilever has tip deflection $W L^3/3EI$.
**Formula.** $\delta=\tfrac{W L^3}{3 E I}$
**Given.** W = 8 kN, L = 4 m, E = 200 GPa, I = 8×10⁻⁶ m⁴.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{8\times10^3\times4^3}{3\times200\times10^9\times8\times10^{-6}}$ m.
**Answer.** $\delta\approx 106.7$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge over a rectangular weir (Cd = 0.62) of length 2 m under a head of 0.3 m is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Discharge over a sharp-crested rectangular weir varies with the 3/2 power of the head.
**Formula.** $Q=\tfrac23 C_d\sqrt{2g}\,L\,H^{3/2}$
**Given.** C_d = 0.62, L = 2 m, H = 0.3 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac23\times0.62\times\sqrt{2\times9.81}\times2\times0.3^{1.5}$.
**Answer.** $Q\approx 0.6$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The strain energy stored per unit volume under axial stress σ is:
questions.solution: Resilience (strain energy density) = σ²/2E.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The method best suited for analysing trusses with a single redundant member is the:
questions.solution: Consistent deformation (unit load) handles internally redundant trusses efficiently.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a balanced section with Fe415 steel, the limiting neutral axis depth factor xu,max/d is:
questions.solution: IS 456 specifies xu,max/d = 0.48 for Fe415 steel.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lug angles in a steel tension member are provided to:
options: Reduce the length of the end connection
questions.solution: Lug angles add connecting area so the gusset connection length is shortened.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shear strength of a cohesionless soil is governed by:
questions.solution: For sands, shear strength = σ' tanφ (no cohesion).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The hydraulic mean depth of a circular pipe running full of diameter D is:
questions.solution: For a full circular pipe, hydraulic radius R = A/P = D/4.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The error due to the curvature of the earth in levelling is:
questions.solution: Earth's curvature makes distant points read lower; correction is +0.0785 d² m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape of the ideal transition curve used on highways is a:
questions.solution: An ideal transition curve is a spiral where radius varies inversely with length.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most common index organism for faecal contamination of water is:
questions.solution: E. coli is used as the indicator of faecal pollution in water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The compound mainly responsible for early strength gain in cement is:
questions.solution: C3S hydrates rapidly and gives early strength to cement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape factor of a rectangular cross-section in plastic bending is:
questions.solution: Shape factor = plastic modulus / elastic section modulus = 1.5 for a rectangle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape factor of a solid circular section in plastic bending is about:
questions.solution: For a solid circular section the shape factor is about 1.7 (16/3π).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The partial safety factor for concrete in the limit state method (IS 456) is:
questions.solution: γm for concrete is 1.5 in limit state design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The partial safety factor for steel in the limit state method (IS 456) is:
questions.solution: γm for reinforcing steel is 1.15 in limit state design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 456, the minimum grade of concrete for reinforced concrete work is:
questions.solution: IS 456 specifies M20 as the minimum grade for RCC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A beam has overall depth 500 mm, clear cover 25 mm and 20 mm main bars. The effective depth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 456, the maximum spacing of main bars in a slab is the smaller of:
questions.solution: Maximum spacing of main reinforcement is 3 times effective depth or 300 mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The development length Ld of a bar is given by:
questions.solution: Ld = φσs/(4τbd), where φ is bar diameter and τbd is design bond stress.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Compression steel in a doubly reinforced beam is provided when:
options: Mu exceeds the limiting moment Mu,lim
questions.solution: When the design moment exceeds the section's balanced (limiting) capacity, compression steel is added.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The cross-sectional area of one 16 mm diameter bar is approximately:
questions.solution: Area = π/4 × 16² = 201 mm².

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The characteristic yield strength of Fe415 reinforcing steel is:
questions.solution: Fe415 denotes a yield strength of 415 MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lateral ties in an RCC column primarily prevent:
questions.solution: Ties hold the main bars in position and prevent their lateral buckling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most economical rolled section for a steel beam in bending is the:
questions.solution: The I-section concentrates material in the flanges, giving high section modulus per unit weight.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 800, the maximum slenderness ratio for a load-bearing steel compression member is:
questions.solution: The limiting slenderness ratio for members carrying loads is 180.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A weld used to join two overlapping plates is a:
questions.solution: Fillet welds are used at the edges of overlapping or lapped plates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lug angles in a steel tension member are provided to:
options: Reduce the length of the end connection
questions.solution: Lug angles add connecting area so the gusset connection length is shortened.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The limit state method of design checks both:
questions.solution: Limit state design verifies the limit states of collapse (strength) and serviceability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Soil with particle size less than 0.002 mm is classified as:
questions.solution: Particles finer than 0.002 mm are classified as clay.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The relation between porosity n and void ratio e is:
questions.solution: By definition, n = e/(1+e).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The plasticity index of a soil is:
questions.solution: Plasticity Index = Liquid Limit − Plastic Limit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If LL = 50% and PL = 30%, the plasticity index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Darcy's law states that the discharge velocity v equals:
questions.solution: Darcy's law: v = ki, where k is permeability and i is hydraulic gradient.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Standard Proctor test is used to determine:
options: Optimum moisture content and maximum dry density
questions.solution: The Proctor compaction test gives the OMC and maximum dry unit weight.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Terzaghi's one-dimensional theory deals with the:
questions.solution: Terzaghi's theory describes one-dimensional consolidation of saturated soils.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The specific gravity of soil solids is typically about:
questions.solution: The specific gravity of most soil solids is around 2.65.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A quick sand condition occurs when the effective stress becomes:
questions.solution: When upward seepage reduces effective stress to zero, the soil loses strength (quick condition).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Rankine active earth pressure coefficient Ka for a soil with φ = 30° is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Standard Penetration Test (SPT) gives the soil's:
questions.solution: The SPT reports the blow count N used to estimate relative density and strength.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The classical bearing capacity theory for shallow footings was given by:
questions.solution: Terzaghi's bearing capacity theory is the standard for shallow foundations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coefficient of permeability of a soil has units of:
questions.solution: Permeability k has the dimensions of velocity, commonly cm/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a void ratio e = 0.5, the porosity n is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The continuity equation for incompressible steady flow in a pipe is:
questions.solution: Conservation of mass gives A1V1 = A2V2 for incompressible flow.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge through a section of area 2 m² with velocity 3 m/s is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bernoulli's equation is based on the conservation of:
questions.solution: Bernoulli's theorem expresses conservation of energy along a streamline.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The pressure at a depth of 10 m in water (ρ = 1000 kg/m³, g = 9.81 m/s²) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flow in a circular pipe is laminar when the Reynolds number is:
questions.solution: Pipe flow is laminar for Re below roughly 2000.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy grade line lies above the hydraulic grade line by an amount equal to the:
questions.solution: EGL = HGL + velocity head (V²/2g).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The velocity head corresponding to a velocity of 2 m/s is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The centre of pressure on a vertical immersed plane surface lies:
questions.solution: Due to increasing pressure with depth, the centre of pressure is below the centroid.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Manning's formula is used for:
questions.solution: Manning's equation estimates uniform flow velocity in open channels.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a floating body, stable equilibrium requires the metacentric height to be:
questions.solution: A positive metacentric height (M above G) ensures stable equilibrium.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A venturimeter is used to measure:
questions.solution: A venturimeter measures discharge using the pressure difference between throat and inlet.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coefficient of discharge Cd of an orifice equals:
questions.solution: Cd is the product of the coefficient of contraction and coefficient of velocity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The instrument used to measure horizontal and vertical angles is the:
questions.solution: A theodolite measures both horizontal and vertical angles.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The process of determining the relative elevations of points is called:
questions.solution: Levelling determines the relative heights (elevations) of points.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If the fore bearing of a line is 60°, its back bearing is:
questions.solution: Back bearing = fore bearing + 180° = 60° + 180° = 240°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Contour lines on a map join points of:
questions.solution: A contour line connects points of equal elevation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Closely spaced contour lines indicate:
questions.solution: The closer the contours, the steeper the ground slope.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RL of a benchmark is 100.0 m. With back sight 1.5 m and fore sight 2.0 m, the RL of the next point is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fundamental principle of surveying is to work:
options: From the whole to the part
From the part to the whole
questions.solution: Surveying proceeds from whole to part to control accumulation of errors.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A prismatic compass measures bearings in the:
questions.solution: A prismatic compass reads whole circle bearings from 0° to 360°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A total station essentially combines a theodolite with:
questions.solution: A total station integrates an electronic theodolite with an EDM and data recorder.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tacheometry is mainly used to determine:
options: Horizontal distances and elevations without chaining
questions.solution: Tacheometry measures distances and elevations rapidly using stadia readings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The number of links in a 30 m metric chain is:
questions.solution: A 30 m metric chain has 150 links of 0.2 m each.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The permanent adjustment of a level ensures the line of collimation is:
options: Parallel to the axis of the bubble tube
questions.solution: The two-peg test adjusts the line of collimation parallel to the bubble axis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Camber on a road surface is provided mainly for:
questions.solution: Camber lets rainwater drain off the carriageway to the sides.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Superelevation is provided on:
questions.solution: Superelevation counters centrifugal force on horizontal curves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superelevation for a design speed of 60 km/h and radius 200 m (e = V²/127R) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The CBR test is used in the design of:
questions.solution: California Bearing Ratio is used to design flexible pavement thickness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rigid pavements are generally made of:
questions.solution: Rigid pavements use a cement concrete slab that resists loads in flexure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stopping sight distance depends on the driver's reaction time and the:
questions.solution: SSD = lag (reaction) distance + braking distance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The broad gauge of Indian Railways measures:
questions.solution: Indian Broad Gauge is 1676 mm; 1435 mm is standard gauge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Marshall stability test is used for:
questions.solution: The Marshall test determines optimum bitumen content in bituminous mixes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Traffic volume is expressed as:
options: Number of vehicles per unit time
questions.solution: Traffic volume is the number of vehicles crossing a section per unit time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) indicates:
questions.solution: BOD measures the oxygen needed to biodegrade organic matter, indicating organic pollution.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The permissible pH range of drinking water as per IS 10500 is:
questions.solution: IS 10500 specifies a desirable pH of 6.5 to 8.5 for drinking water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The average domestic per-capita water demand in Indian cities is taken as about:
questions.solution: A domestic demand of about 135 lpcd is commonly assumed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coagulant most commonly used in water treatment is:
questions.solution: Alum (aluminium sulphate) is the usual coagulant for removing fine suspended matter.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Disinfection of municipal water supplies is most commonly achieved by:
questions.solution: Chlorination is the standard method for disinfecting drinking water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness in water is caused mainly by salts of:
questions.solution: Dissolved calcium and magnesium salts are the main cause of hardness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The activated sludge process is used for the treatment of:
questions.solution: The activated sludge process is an aerobic biological treatment for sewage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The detention time of a tank of volume 100 m³ treating a flow of 50 m³/h is:
questions.solution: Detention time = Volume / Discharge = 100 / 50 = 2 hours.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentation tanks in water treatment primarily remove:
questions.solution: Sedimentation removes settleable suspended solids by gravity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The initial setting time of ordinary Portland cement should not be less than:
questions.solution: IS specifies a minimum initial setting time of 30 minutes for OPC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slump test on fresh concrete measures its:
questions.solution: The slump test is a measure of the workability (consistency) of fresh concrete.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The standard size of a modular brick (including mortar nominal size 200×100×100) without mortar is:
questions.solution: The actual modular brick size is 190 × 90 × 90 mm; nominal size with mortar is 200 × 100 × 100 mm.
